note: Concept-stratified prompts for the GLM-5.2 concept->routing probe. Each prompt gets a fresh glm run with STATS=<file> dumping the per-layer expert-usage histogram = that prompt's routing fingerprint. Offline we test whether within-concept routing similarity exceeds across-concept (permutation test). The 'sense' groups are minimal-pair sense splits (same lexeme, different concept) = the sharpest concept-not-just-token test, and map directly to the kernel's sense-split concepts (break.shatter/violate/interrupt).

prompts: The glass slipped from her hand and broke into pieces on the floor.
The frozen branch snapped and broke under the weight of the ice.
He dropped the plate and it shattered into fragments at once.
He broke his promise to her and never explained why.
The company broke the terms of the contract without warning.
She broke the rules of the club and was quietly expelled.
They stopped for a short break to rest before continuing the hike.
We took a break from work and walked outside for some air.
After a brief pause the meeting resumed where it had left off.
She sat on the grassy bank of the river and watched the water.
The children played along the muddy bank near the old bridge.
Reeds grew thickly on the far bank of the slow stream.
He deposited the cheque at the bank in the centre of town.
The bank raised interest rates on its savings accounts this year.
She applied to the bank for a loan to buy the small shop.
The dog barked loudly at the mail carrier by the gate.
A grey cat slept in the warm sunlight on the windowsill.
The small bird sang brightly in the tree at dawn.
The company reported strong quarterly earnings to its shareholders.
Interest rates on government bonds rose sharply over the year.
He invested most of his savings in a diversified index fund.
The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is one hundred eighty degrees.
To multiply two matrices, take the dot product of rows and columns.
The derivative of a function measures its instantaneous rate of change.
